title: Edit Change Dialog
description: Unified editor for change **metadata** (name, description, invalidation policy) and **spec scope** (`addSpecIds` / `removeSpecIds` / `dependsOn`). This dialog serves dual purposes: modifying an existing change from its Overview tab via **Edit ChangeŌĆ”**, and creating a brand new change via **New change** in the Command Palette. Uses [`ui:scope-change-confirm-dialog`](../scope-change-confirm-dialog/spec.md) as an in-dialog confirm step when scope changes. Studio UI component: **Edit Change Dialog** (formerly Change Scope Dialog).
context: Unified editor for change **metadata** (name, description, invalidation policy) and **spec scope** (`addSpecIds` / `removeSpecIds` / `dependsOn`). This dialog serves dual purposes: modifying an existing change from its Overview tab via **Edit ChangeŌĆ”**, and creating a brand new change via **New change** in the Command Palette. Uses [`ui:scope-change-confirm-dialog`](../scope-change-confirm-dialog/spec.md) as an in-dialog confirm step when scope changes. Studio UI component: **Edit Change Dialog** (formerly Change Scope Dialog).

rules: When opened without an active change context, the dialog MUST operate in "Create" mode. In this mode, the dialog MUST require a **Change Name** input field. Upon saving, it MUST invoke `createChange` with the provided name, description, invalidation policy, and selected specs/dependencies.

The dialog MUST provide inputs for the change's **Description** (Textarea) and **Invalidation Policy** (shadcn `Select`). These fields MUST be prepopulated with the active change's current values in edit mode.

In edit mode, the dialog MUST open with a visible warning (`role="alert"`) stating that **removing** specs drops scaffolded artifact directories and **invalidates** spec approval and sign-off; **adding** specs may require new artifact work; **dependency** edits affect compiled context only and do not invalidate approvals. This warning MUST NOT be shown in creation mode.

rules.requirement: each spec is one card with scope remove and dependencies
rules:
The dialog MUST list each spec in scope as a shadcn **`Card`** (ascending order per [`ui:design-system`](../design-system/spec.md)): header shows `specId` and Ō£Ģ to **remove the spec from scope** (using a shadcn **`Button`**); body shows `dependsOn` as add/remove chips implemented with shadcn **`Badge`** components following the same ordering rule.

**Add spec** and **Add dep** MUST be implemented using a high-performance **`RemoteMultiCombobox`** component:

- The component MUST be built using **Base UI** primitives (`Combobox`, `ComboboxChips`, etc.) integrated into Studio's shadcn theme.
- It MUST support **remote fetching** (debounced 300-500ms) using the `searchGraph` API.
- Selected items MUST appear as **visual chips** within or immediately below the combobox trigger.
- The trigger MUST include a **Search** icon and a **Clear** button (when items are selected).
- Selection MUST NOT be final until the user clicks the adjacent **"Add Spec"** or **"Add dep"** button, which performs the batch update.
- The dialog MUST NOT add a spec as its own dependency. There MUST NOT be a separate chip row duplicating the card list.

rules.requirement: dialog persists scope, dependsOn, and metadata on save
rules: In edit mode, scope and metadata changes use `patchChange`; per-spec dependency changes use `updateSpecDependencies(name, { specId, set: [...] })`. In creation mode, `createChange` is used, followed by `updateSpecDependencies`.

rules.requirement: scope save uses PATCH and confirm step
rules: When draft scope differs from persisted `specIds`, **Save changes** MUST show the confirm sub-step (see `ui:scope-change-confirm-dialog`) then `patchChange` with `addSpecIds` / `removeSpecIds`. Dependency-only or metadata-only saves MUST skip the confirm sub-step. Creation mode MUST skip the confirm sub-step entirely.

`data-testid="studio-change-scope-dialog"`. Large layout (`max-w-2xl`) with scrollable body using shadcn `ScrollArea`. The dialog title MUST indicate the active mode (e.g., "Create new change") and, in edit mode, MUST explicitly include the name of the change being edited (e.g., "Edit change: feat-user-auth"). Actions: **Cancel**, **Save changes**; confirm sub-step: **Back**, **Apply scope change**. Actions MUST use shadcn `Button` components.
MUST use `usePatchChange` and `port.updateSpecDependencies`; MUST NOT import `@specd/core`.

scenarios.name: Dialog initializes for creation
then: change name input is rendered
AND save action invokes createChange port method

scenarios.name: Dialog allows editing schema and invalidation policy
when: user modifies the schema selection or invalidation policy text
then: internal state updates
AND save action persists these metadata fields

scenarios.name: Warning visible on open
then: an alert banner mentions invalidate and removing specs

scenarios.requirement: each spec is one card with scope remove and dependencies
scenarios.name: Add spec form is before spec cards
then: the Add spec field and button appear above studio-change-scope-spec-cards
AND only one datalist#studio-scope-dialog-spec-suggestions exists in the dialog

scenarios.requirement: each spec is one card with scope remove and dependencies
scenarios.name: Add spec creates a new card
given: user types a valid spec ID and submits Add spec
when: ID is not already in draft
then: a new spec card appears with empty dependencies

scenarios.requirement: each spec is one card with scope remove and dependencies
scenarios.name: Add dep uses shared spec suggestions
when: user focuses depends-on on a spec card
then: that input uses list="studio-scope-dialog-spec-suggestions"
AND browser autocomplete can offer default:architecture like Add spec

scenarios.requirement: each spec is one card with scope remove and dependencies
scenarios.name: Self-dependency is ignored
when: user submits Add dep with ui:demo
then: no dependency chip is added for that spec

scenarios.requirement: each spec is one card with scope remove and dependencies
scenarios.name: Remove spec via card header
when: user clicks Ō£Ģ on the card header
then: that card is removed from the list until Save

scenarios.name: Add dependency on the same card
then: dependency chip appears in that cardŌĆÖs body

scenarios.name: Scope PATCH on confirm
given: user confirmed a scope change on the confirm sub-step
then: patchChange runs with addSpecIds and removeSpecIds
AND dialog closes on success

scenarios.requirement: dialog persists scope, dependsOn, and metadata on save
scenarios.name: Dependency PATCH without scope change
then: updateSpecDependencies runs for each edited spec
AND scope PATCH is not invoked

scenarios.requirement: scope save uses PATCH and confirm step
scenarios.name: Scope change shows confirm sub-step
given: user removed a spec from draft
then: dialog shows confirm copy with add/remove lists
AND PATCH runs only after Apply scope change

scenarios.requirement: scope save uses PATCH and confirm step
scenarios.name: Dependencies only skips confirm
given: scope unchanged and only dependsOn edited
then: updateSpecDependencies runs without confirm sub-step

scenarios.name: Dialog uses ScrollArea and Button components
then: it renders with a max-w-2xl layout
AND the scrollable body uses shadcn ScrollArea
AND the action buttons (Cancel, Save changes, Back, Apply scope change) render as shadcn Button components

scenarios.name: No core import
then: ChangeScopeDialog does not import @specd/core
